Enterprise Manager Database Replay Executive Summary

soerep_2

soerep_2 finished with status COMPLETED. Overall verdict: mixed.

WARN Bottom line: mixed
Risk Rating
Low
Replay Database
CDB$ROOT(23.26.1.0.0)
Capture Database
PDB1(21.3.0.0.0)

Executive Summary

Replay "soerep_2" completed successfully. This replay validates an upgrade path from captureVersion 21.3.0.0.0 to replayVersion 23.26.1.0.0. However, performance comparability is limited because DB Time and CPU Time comparison metrics are not available, and capture workload validation is unavailable because the Database Capture Report is missing.

Replay At-a-Glance

  • Verdict: degraded
  • Test outcome: COMPLETED (valid: Yes) - Replay completed and the core comparability checks do not show a major fidelity failure.
  • Test objective: Version upgrade - Capture ran on 21.3.0.0.0 and replay ran on 23.26.1.0.0.
  • DB Time: n/a -> n/a
  • Replay Divergence: Capture baseline -> n/a
  • Functional assessment: Usable.
  • Performance assessment: Mixed (Mixed).

Functional Assessment

Status is Usable. Divergence is 0.00% (Good). No material unreplayable-call sources detected in capture report. Capture report is not available, so workload representativeness cannot be verified.

  • Assessment status: Usable
  • Divergence: 0.00% (Good)
  • Localized divergence: Yes
  • Error source profile: No material unreplayable-call sources detected in capture report.
  • Capture validity: Unknown - Database Capture Report is missing.
Show details

  • Capture report is not available, so workload representativeness cannot be verified.
  • Replay divergence is 0.00% (Good).
  • No material unreplayable-call sources detected in capture report.

Capture Evidence

  • Insufficient data

Actions

  • Functional fidelity is acceptable for decision-making, with noted caveats if any.
  • Include Database Capture Report in future runs to validate captured workload quality.

Performance Assessment

Status is Mixed. DB Time outcome is insufficient data. Dominant mode is mixed.

  • DB Time outcome: Insufficient data
  • Dominant runtime mix (2nd period): Mixed
  • Session completion: Stable

Highlights

  • DB Time: n/a -> n/a
  • CPU Time: n/a -> n/a

Top Bottlenecks (ADDM Comparison Insights)

  • Insufficient data

Key Findings

  • Replay status: COMPLETED.
  • Upgrade validation: captureVersion 21.3.0.0.0 to replayVersion 23.26.1.0.0.
  • DB Time comparison: insufficient data (dbTimeChangePct/dbTimeCapture/dbTimeReplay not provided).
  • CPU Time comparison: insufficient data (cpuTimeChangePct and related CPU metrics not provided).
  • Functional comparability signal: divergencePct is null, so replay divergence cannot be quantified; selectRowFetchDiffCount is 7.
  • Risk rating: Low; overall bottom line flagged as mixed.

Likely Causes

  • Insufficient data: key performance comparison fields (DB Time/CPU) are not populated and the Database Capture Report is not included.

Actions

  • Prioritize DB Time drivers first; do not use elapsed wall-clock time alone.
  • ADDM comparison data is limited; validate with additional diagnostics.
  • Top SQL by DB Time is unavailable in Compare Period report.

Final Verdict

⚠️ Degraded or mixed test result
Replay status and fidelity checks are sufficient for a directional conclusion.
Why this result was assigned:
  • Replay completed and the core comparability checks do not show a major fidelity failure.
  • Capture ran on 21.3.0.0.0 and replay ran on 23.26.1.0.0.
  • Performance assessment for DB Time: Insufficient data.
  • Divergence assessment: Good.

Low

No material issues flagged

The available reports do not show a clear regression signal. Review the underlying reports for environment-specific nuances.

Prototype Verdict Criteria

This section explains the prototype-only scoring logic used to derive the Bottom Line and Risk Rating. It is intended as reviewer guidance and methodology, not as part of the replay performance findings themselves.

How Bottom Line Is Defined

Show details

This prototype uses a rule-based scoring approach, not an LLM, to assign the Bottom Line. Reviewers can use the table below to see the explicit threshold logic.

OutcomePrototype Rule
PASS / goodAssigned when replay is functionally usable and DB Time improves materially, with divergence below 5% or clearly localized/non-critical.
WARN / mixedAssigned when outcome is mixed or degraded: moderate divergence (5-20%), marginal DB Time movement, or unresolved comparability caveats.
FAIL / badAssigned when replay is invalid, including high divergence (>20%), failed/incomplete replay, or severe comparability issues.

How Risk Rating Is Defined

Show details

Risk Rating is a separate roll-up of diagnostic findings. Each finding is assigned a severity and converted into a score:

  • High finding = 35 points
  • Moderate finding = 20 points
  • Low finding = 8 points

The total score is then mapped to the displayed Risk Rating:

Risk RatingPrototype Rule
LowTotal finding severity score below 60. Usually means only a few low or moderate issues were detected.
ModerateTotal finding severity score from 60 to 84. Usually means multiple moderate issues or one high issue plus other concerns were detected.
HighTotal finding severity score 85 or above. Usually means several high-impact issues were detected in the replay analysis.
